content: The original Self-Refine framework, which relies on single-perspective iterative self-feedback to refine outputs without extra training, can be modified to integrate multiple inconsistent solving perspectives drawn from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to eliminate blind spots in single-pass self-evaluation. This hybrid framework would retain Self-Refine’s key advantage of requiring no supervised training or additional reinforcement learning while resolving the common failure mode of repeated self-feedback entrenching incorrect initial outputs.
